secondary_research_themes: NLP and Transformer Applications in SE
methodological_traits: Rigorous manual validation of automated heuristics
communication_indicators: Focus on data validity and noise reduction
researcher_summary: Alexander Trautsch is an empirical software engineering researcher who specializes in critically evaluating and improving the datasets and tools used by the community. Their work frequently points out flaws in standard heuristics (like the SZZ algorithm) and emphasizes the need for manual validation and clean data (untangling commits). They also heavily research the actual impact of static analysis tools on long-term software quality. Recently, they have applied their benchmarking methodology to NLP models and AI generators in software engineering, maintaining a strict focus on rigorous, large-scale empirical evaluation.
